MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-04-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY MOTION AND MOTION TO TRANSFER TO FRESNO COUNTY CHANGE OF VENUE IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 25, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. COURT CONTINUES THE MOTIONS AND ALLOWS COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFFS TO FILE A BRIEF ADDRESSING THE QUESTION AS TO WHY THE CITED SENTENCE IN 395A AND LA FORTUNA DOESN'T LIMIT THE HOLDING OF EASTON ONLY WHERE THERE IS A NONRESIDENT DEFENDANT. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF MUST FILE SAID BRIEF BY OCTOBER 18. THERE SHALL BE NO FURTHER BRIEFING OTHER THAN THE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION THAT IS BEING ALLOWED, AND ONLY ON THIS ONE ARGUMENT. AS TO A FIRST-AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT WAS FILED AROUND THE 2ND OF SEPTEMBER, IT IS BOTH THE COURT'S AND COUNSELS' UNDERSTANDING THAT SINCE THIS MOTION IS STILL PENDING, THE COURT'S JURISDICTION ON THIS ACTION WOULD BE SUSPENDED UNTIL THE MOTION IS RULED UPON. THE TIME FOR ANSWERS OR RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS ON BEHALF OF FORD OR DEALERSHIP WOULD ALSO BE SUSPENDED UNTIL THE COURT RULES ON THE MOTION TO TRANSFER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Defendant Ford Motor Company Motion To Transfer To Fresno County Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Oct-25-2016 Off Calendar

Motion And Motion To Transfer To Fresno County Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to transfer venue is denied. San Francisco County is a proper venue since Ford has not established a California principal place of business and is therefore subject to venue in any county in California. (Easton v. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246). Case law establishes that when a non-California corporation without a principal place of business in California is sued and thus the "any county" venue rule applies, an individual defendant residing in California but not in the county where the lawsuit was filed has the right to change venue to his or her county of residence. (Fitzpatrick v. Sonoma County (1929) 97 Cal. App. 588, 596; see also Brown v. Superior Court (1984) 37 Cal. 3d 477, 483-84, n. 6 ("when a plaintiff brings an action against several defendants, both individual and corporate, in a county in which none of the defendants reside, an individual defendant has the right to change venue to the county of his or her residence.")). Ford argues that, when a California corporation who does not have its principal place of business in the county where the lawsuit is filed is a co-defendant with a non-California corporation that does not have its principal place of business in California, the case must be transferred to the county where the California corporation has its principal place of business. Although there is dicta supporting this argument in the 110 year old case of Ludington Exploration Company v. La Fortuna Gold and Silver Mining Company (1906) 4 Cal. App. 369, 370, no California case so holds nor has any California case ever applied such a rule. Nor is the proposition advanced by Ford mentioned in any of the leading California treatises on venue. Since it is common for a California corporation to be sued along with a non-California corporation, the absence of any case applying the rule advocated by Ford or any treatise referring to such a rule is telling. Ford argues that the "any county" venue rule is an interpretation of the sentence in CCP 395(a) providing that "If none of the defendants reside in the state'the action may be tried in'any county" and thus the any county venue rule is inapplicable unless no defendant, either individual or corporate, resides in California. While this argument has superficial appeal and is supported by the dicta in Ludington, no case has ever adopted this position. The better interpretation of that sentence, and the one consistent with the footnote in Brown, is that the word "reside" in the sentence "If none of the defendants reside in the state'the action may be tried in'any county" refers only to individual defendants who have a "residence" and excludes corporations who, instead of a residence for purposes of CCP 395(a), have a "principal place of business" for purposes of CCP 395.5. Ford's argument that its motion should be granted based on CCP 395(b) and the interests of justice are improper because they were made for the first time in the reply brief and, in any event, those arguments lack merit. As indicated in Fontaine v. Superior Court (2009) 175 Cal. App. 4th 830, the sole case cited by Ford for its invocation of 395(b), that statute was intended to provide broader rights to a consumer plaintiff, not restrict them, as Ford seeks to do. "Interests of justice" is a standard used for a motion to change venue due to witness convenience, but is inapplicable to a motion asserting that the case was filed in the wrong county. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Motion And Motion To Transfer To Fresno County Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Continued To Oct-25-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Christine Lee of Strategic Legal Practices APC (310.929.4900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Michael Foley of Gordon Rees LLP (415.986.5900) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY Motion And Motion To Transfer To Fresno County Change Of Venue is CONTINUED TO October 25, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. Court continues the motions and allows counsel for the plaintiffs to file a brief addressing the question as to why the cited sentence in 395A and La Fortuna doesn't limit the holding of Easton only where there is a nonresident defendant. Counsel for plaintiff must file said brief by October 18. There shall be no further briefing other than the supplemental opposition that is being allowed, and only on this one argument. As to a first-amended complaint that was filed around the 2nd of September, it is both the Court's and counsels' understanding that since this motion is still pending, the Court's jurisdiction on this action would be suspended until the motion is ruled upon. The time for answers or responsive pleadings on behalf of Ford or dealership would also be suspended until the Court rules on the motion to transfer.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
